get.description: Return engine and client compatibility version info.

Return indicator registry with parameter schemas + strategy-logic
metadata. Response is filtered by the caller's scopes: admin-scoped
keys see every registered indicator; non-admin keys see only
`tier="public"` items (see docs/AUTH_SPEC.md §4.1).

Extended in Phase F.1 (STRATEGY_LOGIC_BACKEND_SPEC.md §6.1) to expose
`kind`, `arity`, `value_dtype`, `output_columns`, `tags`, plus a
top-level `version` SHA-256 over registry contents for client cache
invalidation.

Return the JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) for the Strategy document.

Frontends consume this for client-side validation, form generation,
and IDE tooling. Schema is a static module-level constant — see
src/backtest360_engine/api/strategy_schema.py.

post.description:
Validate a Strategy document without running a backtest.

Body shape (per STRATEGY_LOGIC_BACKEND_SPEC.md §6.3):
    {
      "strategy": <Strategy doc per §5>,
      "scope": {"leaf_paths": [...]}      # optional fast path
    }

Returns 200 with {valid: true, warmup_bars, referenced_indicators,
referenced_columns_per_slot, warnings: []} on success, or 422 with
{valid: false, errors: [...]} on failure. Errors carry a structured
`code` + JSON-Pointer `location` per Phase C / backend spec §7.

get.description: Return condition operators for the Strategy Creator.

Return the strategies available to the caller.

Response shape:
  {
    "strategies": [
      {"id": ..., "origin": "system" | "user", "name": ..., ...},
      ...
    ]
  }

`origin='system'` items come from auto-discovery of
`src/backtest360_engine/strategies/templates/`. `origin='user'` items would be
loaded from the platform's per-user store given a `user_id` — not yet
wired here; returns only system templates for now.

Filtered by caller scopes per docs/AUTH_SPEC.md §4.1 — admin-scoped
keys see every strategy; non-admin keys see only `tier="public"`
strategies.

Return valid execution anchors and fill algorithms.

Args:
    is_24h: Whether the market trades 24h (e.g., crypto). Default True.
    anchor: If provided, return valid fill algorithms for this anchor only.
Return section -> field mappings for performance stats.
Return MC sampling modes with descriptions and per-mode parameters.
Return stop loss types with per-type parameters.
Return position sizing methods with parameters.

Return the full server-wide bar frequency capability set plus the
hard signal-x-execution compatibility matrix the GUI MUST enforce.

The matrix encodes the engine's 18 supported (signal_frequency,
execution_bar_frequency) combinations. The GUI filters its execution
dropdown based on the selected signal using the ``valid_execution_bars``
map. It also toggles the ``open_hour``/``close_hour`` controls based on
``open_close_hour_applies_when`` — those fields are only meaningful in
exactly one scenario: daily signal + hourly execution.

post.description: Receive a file, call parse_backtest_file(), return ParseResult as JSON.

get.description: List all available tickers, optionally filtered by asset class.
Look up a single asset by ticker.
Search for assets by name or ticker via provider.
Get available date range and market hours for a symbol.

post.description: Fetch OHLCV data via configured provider + cache, return summary + price series.
Run backtest via new pipeline, return full result as JSON.

Return the strategy's latest signal for the most recent bar of OHLCV.

Same request shape as /api/backtest. The engine slices market_data to
the warmup tail (per Strategy.required_warmup_bars), runs signal
generation only, returns the last bar's signal int plus the latest
indicator panels (no P&L, no statistics, no equity curve).

Used for live trading and "what would the strategy say right now?"
GUI buttons.
Same request shape as /api/backtest — returns dummy results instantly.
Run multiple strategies against same data, return side-by-side comparison.
Compute 120+ performance metrics from a return series.
Run Monte Carlo simulation synchronously. Returns full result.

Compute historical result's percentile rank within MC distribution.

Stateless: caller provides both the MC result and historical stats inline.

EngineConfig.title: EngineConfig
EngineConfig.description: Legacy — kept for Monte Carlo endpoint (deferred).

ExecutionConfig.title: ExecutionConfig
ExecutionConfig.description: Execution, costs, risk, sizing, timing — all in one.

OHLCVInput.title: OHLCVInput
OHLCVInput.description: Inline OHLCV data as parallel arrays.

StatsTradeInput.title: StatsTradeInput
StatsTradeInput.description:
Single trade record for /api/stats. Accepts the same shape as
run_result.trades produced by run_backtest.

StrategyInput.title: StrategyInput
StrategyInput.description:
Strategy definition — condition_tree + indicators (per ADR-003).

`condition_tree` is a json-logic-style bundle with four nullable
slots (long_entry / long_exit / short_entry / short_exit), each a
tree of and/or/not/leaf nodes. `indicators` is an ordered DAG of
ResolvedIndicator dicts referenced by tree leaves via `ref`.
